title: Add CodeCov to travis
body:
Signed-off-by: TomSweeneyRedHat <tsweeney@redhat.com>

Adding CodeCov to generate Code Coverage numbers to Buildah.

comments.body: It seems like the CI failure is unrelated to this change.
@TomSweeneyRedHat Is this giving us any useful information?

It's doing nothing at the moment.  I thought I'd find a link to the report on codecov.io that would basically be blank or next to blank.  But it's not there.  I contacted codecov.io support Thursday, haven't heard back yet.  I was going to try and add some instrumentation to one of our tests today to see if that massaged it along further.
Going to close and reopen this.  For some reason travis didn't leave any bread crumbs behind.
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably d7fe23e) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.
@TomSweeneyRedHat Did you ever get this to give us some benefit, or should we just close.

@rhatdan no benny yet.  The latest issue that I'm trying to solve is the coverage report.  It appears to be getting created in the container during the build process, but the one that's retained at the end of it all in travis is empty.  Trying to figure that out, but haven't looked into it for a bit now.
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably 59b1ada) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.
I'd massive issues in my sandbox.  I'm closing this as it was easier to restart in #964
